title: Theory of Computation - Recursive and Recursively Enumerable Languages

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is false?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 63
options: The set of all languages that are not recursively enumerable is countable
The family of recursively enumerable language is closed under union
The families of recursively enumerable and recursive languages are closed under reversal
questions.correct_answer: The set of all languages that are not recursively enumerable is countable

questions.question:
Given the following two statements:

\( S_1 \): If \( L_1 \) and \( L_2 \) are recursively enumerable languages over \( \Sigma^* \), then \( L_1 \cup L_2 \) and \( L_1 \cap L_2 \) are also recursively enumerable.

\( S_2 \): The set of recursively enumerable languages is countable.

Which of the following is true?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 62
options: \( S_1 \) is correct and \( S_2 \) is not correct
\( S_1 \) is not correct and \( S_2 \) is correct
Both \( S_1 \) and \( S_2 \) are not correct

questions.question:
Consider \( L=L_1 \cap L_2 \) where

\( L_1 = \{ 0^m 1^m 20^n 1^n \mid m,n \geq 0 \} \)

\( L_2 = \{0^m1^n2^k \mid m,n,k \geq 0 \} \)

Then, the language \( L \) is

 



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 27
